content.full_text:
April 17, 1990

Alice Hughes  
California Fair Political Practices Commission  
1100 K Street  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804  

Re: Ellen Chaitin For Judge Committee  
ID # 900446  

Dear Ms. Hughes:

This is to follow up on our telephone conversations of yesterday and today during which I requested an informal opinion on whether a candidate for a Municipal Court judgeship is a "county" candidate or a "state" candidate for campaign contribution limitation purposes. I now respectfully request a written opinion confirming the conclusions stated during our discussion.

By way of background, I am the treasurer of the committee listed above. Ms. Chaitin is a duly qualified candidate for a judgeship in the Municipal Court of California for the City and County of San Francisco. The court is established by California Constitution Article 6, Section 5. California Government Code section 71001, et seq., govern the administration of court and the creation by the Legislature of districts within counties.

The City and County of San Francisco has a campaign contribution limitation ordinance entitled the San Francisco Municipal Election Campaign Contribution Control Ordinance. This law sets stricter limits on campaign contributions than does existing state law. For example, candidates for municipal office are limited to a maximum contribution of $500 per contributor in any municipal election other than a run-off election. In a run-off election an additional $250 is permitted.

The issue presented by this request is whether or not a candidate for Municipal Court judge in San Francisco is subject to the San Francisco ordinance limiting contributions to candidates for county offices.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this request for a

Alice Hughes  
California Fair Political Practices Commission  
April 17, 1990  
Page two

ruling.

Please let me know if you have any questions regarding the foregoing.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

DONALD DISLER,
Treasurer,
Ellen Chaitin for Judge Committee

DD/bh

cc: Ellen Chaitin
